request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will 25+ FATF member states formally adopt updated VASP Travel Rule guidance by 2027-06-30?
Kind: active
Timeline (20 events):
- 2026-05-22 [background] FIL-24-2026 - 22.05.2026 - Notice of Proposed Rulemaking to Establish Bank Secrecy Act and Sanctions Compliance Standards for FDIC-Supervised Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuers — FDIC NPRM imposing BSA/sanctions standards on stablecoin issuers signals the US may adopt Travel‑Rule‑style measures, modestly raising YES odds.
- 2026-05-12 [A] FINMA eröffnet Anhörung zur Teilrevision der Geldwäschereiverordnung-FINMA — FINMA's consultation to revise Switzerland's AML ordinance suggests likely transposition of FATF Rec‑15, slightly raising probability 25+ members comply by 2027‑06‑30.
- 2026-05-12 [background] FINMA launches consultation on partial revision of the FINMA Anti-Money Laundering Ordinance — FINMA's AML-ordinance consultation signals Switzerland is moving to transpose FATF Rec‑15 updates, a small bullish signal toward reaching 25+ transpositions.
- 2026-05-07 [background] DOWNLOAD 130.35 KB — An ESMA/EC-level document likely signals EU-wide guidance or rulemaking to align with FATF Rec‑15, modestly raising chances many EU members transpose it.
- 2026-05-05 [background] DOWNLOAD 553.18 KB — An ESMA/NCAs publication could signal EU coordination on VASP/Travel‑Rule transposition, slightly increasing odds that multiple FATF members adopt the update.
- 2026-05-04 [background] ESMA advances the simplification of EU reporting frameworks for funds and transactions - Press release — ESMA's simplification of EU reporting may ease national transposition of VASP‑adjacent rules by EU members, slightly boosting odds but is indirect.
- 2026-04-13 [background] Dear Tribal Leader Letter - AML/CFT NPRM 2026 - 10.04.2026 — FinCEN's AML/CFT NPRM indicates the U.S. is advancing domestic implementation efforts, slightly raising the chance one FATF member transposes the Rec‑15 update.
- 2026-04-10 [background] Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuer Anti-Money Laundering/Countering the Financing of Terrorism Program and Sanctions Compliance Program Requirements — US Treasury's Apr 2026 stablecoin AML/CFT rule implies the U.S. likely transposed the Rec‑15 update, increasing odds FATF will later confirm ≥25 members.
- 2026-04-10 [background] NPRM: Anti-Money Laundering and CFT Programs - 10.04.2026 — FinCEN NPRM signals the US is moving to implement FATF Rec‑15 changes, modestly raising the odds the US will count toward the 25+ transpositions.
- 2026-04-10 [background] 04/10/26 - Anti-Money Laundering and Countering the Financing of Terrorism Programs (RIN 3064–AF34) — U.S. OCC AML/CFT rulemaking suggests the U.S. is transposing FATF Rec‑15, a small positive toward reaching 25+ FATF member confirmations.
- 2026-04-08 [background] Treasury Proposes Rule to Implement the GENIUS Act’s Requirements to Counter Illicit Finance — FinCEN's proposed GENIUS Act rule shows the U.S. (a FATF member) is moving to implement new anti‑illicit‑finance rules, modestly raising odds of U.S. transposition.
- 2026-04-08 [background] Treasury Proposes Rule to Implement the GENIUS Act's Requirements to Counter Illicit Finance — FinCEN's proposed GENIUS Act rule signals the US moving to implement expanded VASP controls, modestly raising odds the US will transpose FATF Rec‑15.
- 2026-04-07 [background] Issuance of a new Anti-Money Laundering (AML)/Countering the Financing of Terrorism (CFT) Program Requirements Notice of Proposed Rulemaking — FDIC AML/CFT NPRM signals the U.S. moving toward adopting VASP-related rules, modestly increasing odds the U.S. will count toward 25 transpositions.
- 2026-04-07 [background] Agencies Request Comment on Anti-Money Laundering/Countering the Financing of Terrorism Proposed Rule — Interagency FDIC request for comment on AML/CFT rulemaking signals US movement toward transposing FATF Rec‑15, slightly boosting YES odds.
- 2026-03-30 [background] 30.03.2026 - The European regulatory framework on crypto-asset service providers is complete – last ESMA Guidelines applicable from 28 July 2026 — EU-wide ESMA guidelines for crypto-asset providers (effective 28 Jul 2026) make it likelier many European FATF members will transpose VASP rules before 2027-06-30.
- 2026-02-13 [background] 13.02.2026 - L'AMF invite les acteurs de la Place à répondre à la consultation de l'AMLA sur des projets de normes d'application en matière de LCB-FT — EU/AMLA consultation signals coordinated EU implementation work that raises likelihood many EU FATF members will transpose Rec‑15 into domestic rules.
- 2026-01-13 [background] Geographic Targeting Order Imposing Recordkeeping and Reporting Requirements on Certain Financial Institutions in Minnesota — FinCEN's GTO indicates U.S. domestic adoption of expanded VASP recordkeeping/reporting, slightly raising chance the U.S. will count toward FATF confirmations.
- 2025-12-30 [background] Autoritatea de Supraveghere Financiară -Publicarea regulamentelor (UE) 2025/2179 și 2025/2180 în Jurnalul Oficial al Uniunii Europene — EU regulations published in the OJ imply bloc‑wide legal transposition of VASP-related rules, raising odds many FATF-member EU states meet the criterion.
- 2025-12-18 [background] AMLA takes major step toward harmonised EU supervision — AMLA move toward harmonised EU supervision raises odds the EU/its members will adopt FATF Rec‑15, increasing chance of reaching 25+ confirmed transpositions.
- 2025-11-09 [background] MiCA In-depth Guide - 29.11.2024 — MiCA in‑depth guide indicates coordinated EU-level VASP rules, raising likelihood many EU FATF members will align with the Rec‑15 update.

response.text: Momentum builds as the EU advances harmonized VASP/Travel Rule implementation (AMLA, OJ regs, ESMA guidelines) and the U.S. issues multiple AML/CFT NPRMs, including stablecoin and GENIUS Act rules; Switzerland’s FINMA opens an AML ordinance revision. But many actions remain unfinished; final national adoptions and FATF confirmation to reach 25+ members are still pending.
